before.title: Jewish Museum Berlin: Free Entry, How Long and What to See (2026)
imageAltTexts: Baroque Old Building and zinc-clad Libeskind Building of the Jewish Museum Berlin seen from Lindenstrasse.
Entrance sign and baroque Old Building of the Jewish Museum Berlin on Lindenstrasse.
Concrete stelae topped with plants in the Garden of Exile at the Jewish Museum Berlin.
Narrow shaft of daylight entering the concrete Holocaust Tower at the Jewish Museum Berlin.
Display cases, wall text and map inside the current core exhibition at the Jewish Museum Berlin.
Glass Courtyard with tables and the historic facade inside the Jewish Museum Berlin.
before.coverAlt: Baroque Old Building and zinc-clad Libeskind Building of the Jewish Museum Berlin seen from Lindenstrasse.

props.name: description
props.content: Plan the Jewish Museum Berlin: free core exhibition, 2.5–3 hour visit, opening hours, tickets, bags, accessibility and the best Libeskind route.

props.name: robots
props.content: index, follow, max-image-preview:large
Jewish Museum Berlin: The Visit Plan I Would Use
The core exhibition is free, but the building needs time. Use this route for the three axes, Garden of Exile, Holocaust Tower and 1,700 years of Jewish life.
